The reference is necessary because responses within a particular Elo rating bucket are not uniformly distributed across the GPQA questions - some of which are inherently more challenging than others. This non-uniformity could introduce bias into the analysis and potentially lead to erroneous conclusions. We therefore used the reference to generate 32 responses for each GPQA question. The fraction of correct responses from Gemini 2.0 was used as a reference accuracy on that particular question. To determine reference accuracy for a specific Elo bucket, we averaged the reference accuracy of the GPQA questions that had co-scientist responses within that bucket. We also computed the co-scientist accuracy on the GPQA diamond set by using the result with the highest Elo rating for each question and comparing it against the ground truth.
items.zh: 我们在AI共同科学家中使用底层的Gemini 2.0模型创建了一个参考基线。参考是必要的，因为特定Elo评分区间内的响应在GPQA问题中并非均匀分布——有些问题本质上比其他问题更具挑战性。这种不均匀性可能会在分析中引入偏差，并可能导致错误结论。因此，我们使用参考为每个GPQA问题生成32个响应。Gemini 2.0的正确响应比例被用作该特定问题的参考准确率。为了确定特定Elo区间的参考准确率，我们平均了具有该区间内共同科学家响应的GPQA问题的参考准确率。我们还通过使用每个问题中具有最高Elo评分的结果并与真实答案进行比较，计算了共同科学家在GPQA钻石集上的准确率。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Concordance of the auto-evaluation Elo metric with AI co-scientist performance on GPQA. The blue line in the figure shows the average accuracy of co-scientist responses, grouped by their Elo rating. The red line indicates the average accuracy of the corresponding reference Gemini 2.0 responses to the same set of GPQA questions, grouped by Elo rating. Note that Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on the ground truth.
items.capZh: 图3：自动评估Elo指标与AI共同科学家在GPQA上表现的一致性。图中的蓝线显示了按Elo评分分组的共同科学家响应的平均准确率。红线表示相应参考Gemini 2.0对同一组GPQA问题的响应的平均准确率，按Elo评分分组。注意，Elo指标是自动评估的，并非基于真实答案。

items.en: Our analysis using questions from the GPQA diamond set reveals a concordance between the Elo rating and averaged accuracy of generated co-scientist results, as depicted in Figure˜3. By selecting the top-rated co-scientist result for each question, the co-scientist achieves a top-1 accuracy of 78.4%.
items.zh: 我们使用GPQA钻石集中的问题进行的分析揭示了Elo评分与生成的共同科学家结果的平均准确率之间的一致性，如图3所示。通过为每个问题选择评分最高的共同科学家结果，共同科学家达到了78.4%的top-1准确率。

full.sec: 4.2 Scaling test-time compute improves scientific reasoning of the AI co-scientist
full.secZh: 4.2 测试时计算扩展提升人工智能共同科学家的科学推理能力

items.en: To evaluate the effects of test-time compute scaling and the co-scientist’s progress during iterative scientific reasoning and hypothesis generation, we measured the Elo ratings of the co-scientist generated hypothesis and proposals over the course of the tournament. This analysis was done across 203 distinct research goals curated across broad scientific topics (predominantly in biomedicine, but also included other topics such as mathematics and physics) and entered into the co-scientist system until February 3, 2025.
items.zh: 为了评估测试时算力缩放和共同科学家在迭代科学推理和假设生成过程中的进展，我们测量了在锦标赛过程中共同科学家生成的假设和提案的Elo评分。此分析涵盖了203个不同的研究目标，这些目标来自广泛的科学主题（主要是生物医学，但也包括数学和物理等其他主题），并在2025年2月3日之前输入共同科学家系统。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Impact of scaling test-time compute on AI co-scientist as measured by Elo auto-evaluation. The co-scientist’s research hypotheses and proposals were partitioned into ten temporal buckets of equal size, with the last bucket corresponding to the most recently generated results from the system. For each bucket, we determined the maximum individual Elo rating (the “best Elo”) and the average Elo rating of the top 10 hypotheses across 203 unique research goals. The resulting upward performance trends, across both metrics, suggest improvements in the co-scientist result quality with scaling of test-time compute. Note that the Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on independent ground truth.
items.capZh: 图4：测试时算力缩放对AI共同科学家的影响，通过Elo自动评估测量。共同科学家的研究假设和提案被划分为十个大小相等的时间桶，最后一个桶对应系统最近生成的结果。对于每个桶，我们确定了最大个体Elo评分（“最佳Elo”）以及203个独特研究目标中前10个假设的平均Elo评分。两个指标均呈现上升性能趋势，表明随着测试时算力的缩放，共同科学家结果质量有所提高。注意，Elo指标是自动评估的，并非基于独立真实答案。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Comparison of the AI co-scientist with other baselines as measured by Elo auto-evaluation on a subset of 15 challenging expert-curated research goals. The AI co-scientist’s research hypotheses and proposals were partitioned into ten equal-sized temporal buckets, with the last bucket representing the most recent system-generated results. For each bucket, we calculated the maximum individual Elo rating (“best Elo”) and the average Elo rating of the top 10 hypotheses across 15 challenging research goals, curated by biomedical experts. These metrics were then compared to the performance of several baselines: Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental, Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19, OpenAI o1, OpenAI o3-mini-high, DeepSeek R1, and the “best guess” hypotheses provided by the expert curators. These baseline performances are represented by colored dashed lines. On this expert-curated subset of research goals, we consistently observed upward performance trends with increased test-time compute. Furthermore, the co-scientist significantly outperformed both the human experts and the other state-of-the-art LLM baselines, as measured by the Elo metric. It’s important to note that the Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on independent ground truth. This auto-evaluation metric may favour results with attributes that do not necessarily align with scientists’ preferences, quality, or accuracy.
items.capZh: 图5：AI共同科学家与其他基线在15个具有挑战性的专家策划研究目标子集上的比较，通过Elo自动评估测量。AI共同科学家的研究假设和提案被划分为十个大小相等的时间桶，最后一个桶代表系统最近生成的结果。对于每个桶，我们计算了最大个体Elo评分（“最佳Elo”）以及15个具有挑战性的研究目标（由生物医学专家策划）中前10个假设的平均Elo评分。然后将这些指标与几个基线的性能进行比较：Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental、Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19、OpenAI o1、OpenAI o3-mini-high、DeepSeek R1以及专家策划者提供的“最佳猜测”假设。这些基线性能由彩色虚线表示。在这个专家策划的研究目标子集上，我们一致观察到随着测试时算力的增加，性能呈上升趋势。此外，根据Elo指标，共同科学家显著优于人类专家和其他最先进的LLM基线。需要注意的是，Elo指标是自动评估的，并非基于独立真实答案。这个自动评估指标可能偏向于具有不一定符合科学家偏好、质量或准确性的属性的结果。

items.en: The co-scientist’s research hypotheses and proposals were partitioned into ten temporal buckets of equal size. Each bucket corresponded to a sequential 10% of the total generation time with the first bucket containing the earliest 10% of generated co-scientist results, while the tenth bucket comprised the most recent 10%. For each bucket, we determined the average Elo rating of the top 10 hypotheses and the maximum individual Elo rating (the “best Elo”). These average and best Elo ratings were averaged across 203 research goals and their corresponding tournaments. The resulting performance trends as seen in Figure˜4, across both metrics, serves as a measure of the co-scientist’s quality improvement over time. The most recent results demonstrated a significant quality enhancement compared to the initial outputs. Notably, although the Elo rating is not the direct optimization target, its progressive increase emerges from the system’s self-improvement feedback loops.
items.zh: 共同科学家的研究假设和提案被划分为十个大小相等的时间桶。每个桶对应总生成时间的顺序10%，第一个桶包含最早生成的10%的共同科学家结果，而第十个桶包含最近生成的10%。对于每个桶，我们确定了前10个假设的平均Elo评分和最大个体Elo评分（“最佳Elo”）。这些平均和最佳Elo评分在203个研究目标及其对应的锦标赛中取平均。如图4所示的性能趋势，在两个指标上，都作为共同科学家质量随时间改进的度量。最近的结果显示出与初始输出相比显著的质量提升。值得注意的是，尽管Elo评分不是直接的优化目标，但其逐步增加源于系统的自我改进反馈循环。

items.en: To further contextualize this observation, we focused our analysis on a subset of 15 research goals, curated as challenging problems by seven biomedical experts in their respective fields of expertise. These experts held doctoral degrees in biological or life science disciplines and were actively working as postdoctoral researchers or faculty members at academic biomedical research institutes. These research goals were consistently structured and formatted, encompassing a research title, a clear set of goals, preferences specifying biological or disease areas of interest, desirable solution attributes, and constraints on experimental techniques.
items.zh: 为了进一步将这一观察结果置于背景中，我们将分析重点放在15个研究目标的子集上，这些目标由七位生物医学专家在其各自专业领域内策划为具有挑战性的问题。这些专家拥有生物学或生命科学学科的博士学位，并在学术生物医学研究机构担任博士后研究员或教职员工。这些研究目标的结构和格式一致，包括研究标题、明确的目标集、指定感兴趣的生物学或疾病领域的偏好、期望的解决方案属性以及实验技术的约束。

items.en: In addition to the research goals, the experts provided their “best guess” hypotheses or solutions. We then included outputs from other state-of-the-art LLMs and reasoning models—–Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental, Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19, OpenAI o1, OpenAI o3-mini-high, and DeepSeek R1—in a tournament along with the expert “best guess” and co-scientist for each curated goal. Performance was assessed using the co-scientist Elo rating metric.
items.zh: 除了研究目标，专家还提供了他们的“最佳猜测”假设或解决方案。然后，我们将其他最先进的LLM和推理模型——Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental、Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19、OpenAI o1、OpenAI o3-mini-high和DeepSeek R1——的输出与专家“最佳猜测”和共同科学家一起纳入每个策划目标的锦标赛中。使用共同科学家Elo评分指标评估性能。

items.en: The trends previously observed with test-time compute scaling in Figure˜4 were consistent within this subset. Furthermore, as shown in Figure˜5, the co-scientist surpassed the other frontier LLMs and reasoning models in Elo rating with increased computational resources for iterative improvement. Notably, newer reasoning models, such as OpenAI o3-mini-high and DeepSeek R1, demonstrated competitive performance while requiring significantly less compute and reasoning time. Finally, we observed no evidence of performance saturation as measured by Elo, suggesting that further scaling of test-time compute in this paradigm could yield continued improvements in result quality of the co-scientist system. Its worth noting again that the co-scientist architecture is model agnostic and is likely to benefit from further advancements in frontier and reasoning LLMs.
items.zh: 先前在图4中观察到的测试时算力缩放趋势在此子集内是一致的。此外，如图5所示，随着用于迭代改进的计算资源的增加，共同科学家在Elo评分上超过了其他前沿LLM和推理模型。值得注意的是，较新的推理模型，如OpenAI o3-mini-high和DeepSeek R1，在需要显著更少的算力和推理时间的同时，展示了具有竞争力的性能。最后，我们没有观察到由Elo测量的性能饱和的证据，这表明在此范式下进一步缩放测试时算力可能会带来共同科学家系统结果质量的持续改进。再次值得指出的是，共同科学家架构是模型无关的，并且可能受益于前沿和推理LLM的进一步进步。

items.capEn: Figure 6: AI-augmented expertise with the co-scientist through Elo-based auto-evaluation. Through its self-improvement process, the co-scientist refines and enhances expert “best guess” solutions over time, as measured by the Elo rating on a subset of 15 curated research goals. It is important to note that the Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on independent ground truth.
items.capZh: 图6：通过基于Elo的自动评估，AI增强的专家知识与共同科学家。通过其自我改进过程，共同科学家随着时间的推移改进和增强专家“最佳猜测”解决方案，如15个策划研究目标子集上的Elo评分所测量。需要注意的是，Elo指标是自动评估的，并非基于独立真实答案。

items.en: Building upon the co-scientist system’s ability to combine, refine and improve research hypotheses and proposals iteratively, we investigated its potential to improve upon expert “best guess” solutions. Consistent with our previous observations, the co-scientist demonstrated the capacity to enhance expert’s “best guess” solutions over time, as evidenced by the Elo metric in Figure˜6. Notably, the improvement trends initially mirrored those of the co-scientist’s autonomously generated solutions but subsequently surpassed them. While this is a preliminary finding requiring further validation, it suggests a promising avenue for capable AI systems, such as the co-scientist, to augment and accelerate the work of expert scientists.
items.zh: 基于共同科学家系统迭代组合、改进和提升研究假设和提案的能力，我们研究了其改进专家“最佳猜测”解决方案的潜力。与我们之前的观察一致，共同科学家展示了随时间增强专家“最佳猜测”解决方案的能力，如图6中的Elo指标所证明。值得注意的是，改进趋势最初与共同科学家自主生成的解决方案相似，但随后超越了它们。虽然这是一个初步发现，需要进一步验证，但它为像共同科学家这样的有能力的AI系统增强和加速专家科学家的工作提供了一个有前景的途径。

full.sec: 4.3 Experts consider the AI co-scientist results to be potentially novel and impactful
full.secZh: 4.3 专家认为人工智能共同科学家结果具有潜在新颖性和影响力

items.en: To obtain expert feedback and assess preferences, we conducted a small-scale expert evaluation on 11 of the 15 previously curated research goals. We asked the experts who curated the research goals to assess outputs from the AI co-scientist, Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19, Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental, and OpenAI o1 models. Specifically, they provided a preference ranking (1 being most preferred and 4 being least preferred) and rated the novelty and impact of the proposed solutions on a 5-point scale, ranging from 1 (worst) to 5 (best) following this rubric:
items.zh: 为了获取专家反馈并评估偏好，我们对之前策划的15个研究目标中的11个进行了小规模专家评估。我们请策划研究目标的专家评估来自AI共同科学家、Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19、Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental和OpenAI o1模型的输出。具体来说，他们提供了偏好排名（1为最偏好，4为最不偏好），并按照以下评分标准对提出解决方案的新颖性和影响力进行5分制评分（1为最差，5为最好）：

items.en: Novelty: Higher-ranked outputs should propose hypotheses that, to the best of the expert’s knowledge, have not been previously published in any form. Hypotheses similar to existing proposals, even with minor modifications, should rank lower, and exact replicas of previously proposed and performed experiments should receive the lowest ranking.
items.zh: 新颖性：排名较高的输出应提出据专家所知尚未以任何形式发表的假设。与现有提案相似（即使有微小修改）的假设应排名较低，而先前提出并执行的实验的精确复制品应获得最低排名。

items.en: Impact: Higher-ranked outputs should address significant open questions in the field and have the potential to substantially advance scientific understanding or lead to practical applications.
items.zh: 影响：排名较高的输出应解决该领域的重要开放性问题，并具有显著推进科学理解或带来实际应用的潜力。

items.en: Across 11 expert-evaluated research goals, outputs generated by the AI co-scientist were most preferred and rated higher in novelty and impact axes compared to the other baseline models. Specifically, the co-scientist received an average preference rank of 2.36, and novelty and impact ratings of 3.64 and 3.09 (out of 5) as shown in Figure˜7. These evaluations reflect subjective expert assessments, not objective ground truth. Notably, the human expert preferences also appear to be concordant with relative Elo ratings as can be inferred from Figure˜5 and Figure˜7.
items.zh: 在11个专家评估的研究目标中，AI 共同科学家生成的输出在偏好、新颖性和影响力方面均优于其他基线模型。具体而言，共同科学家的平均偏好排名为2.36，新颖性和影响力评分分别为3.64和3.09（满分5分），如图7所示。这些评估反映了主观的专家判断，而非客观事实。值得注意的是，人类专家的偏好与相对 Elo 评分一致，可从图5和图7推断。

items.en: We also conducted the same preference ranking evaluation between co-scientist and other LLM and reasoning model baselines using the OpenAI o3-mini-2025-01-31, o1-preview-2024-09-12, Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental and Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 01-21 as judges. The co-scientist outputs were the most preferred by both the o3-mini, o1 and Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental models as shown in (Figure˜8). Due to the small scale of these evaluations, further large-scale studies are necessary for any reliable conclusions. We present a more comprehensive clinical expert evaluation focused on co-scientist proposals for drug repurposing formatted in the NIH Specific Aims Page format in Section˜4.5.1.
items.zh: 我们还使用 OpenAI o3-mini-2025-01-31、o1-preview-2024-09-12、Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental 和 Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 01-21 作为评判者，对共同科学家与其他 LLM 和推理模型基线进行了相同的偏好排名评估。如图8所示，共同科学家的输出在 o3-mini、o1 和 Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental 模型中均最受青睐。由于这些评估规模较小，需要进一步的大规模研究才能得出可靠结论。我们在第4.5.1节中展示了更全面的临床专家评估，重点关注以 NIH 特定目标页面格式呈现的共同科学家的药物重定位提案。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Expert evaluation of AI co-scientist and other LLM baselines. Left: Average expert ratings on novelty and impact of the model responses across 11 expert curated research goals. Higher numbers indicate better ratings (1-5). Right: Average expert preference ranking of the results across 11 expert curated research goals generated by AI co-scientist, Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19, Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental, and OpenAI o1, respectively. Lower numbers indicate better rankings (1-4). The human expert preferences also appear to be concordant with relative Elo ratings as can be inferred from Figure˜5. At the same time, its worth noting that these preferences and ratings reflect subjective expert assessments, not objective ground truth.
items.capZh: 图7：AI 共同科学家与其他 LLM 基线的专家评估。左图：专家对11个专家策划的研究目标中模型响应的新颖性和影响力的平均评分。数值越高表示评分越好（1-5分）。右图：专家对 AI 共同科学家、Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19、Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental 和 OpenAI o1 生成的结果的平均偏好排名。数值越低表示排名越好（1-4分）。人类专家的偏好与相对 Elo 评分一致，可从图5推断。同时，值得注意的是，这些偏好和评分反映了主观的专家评估，而非客观事实。

items.capEn: Figure 8: LLM preference ranking auto-evaluation of AI co-scientist and other baselines. Averaged preference ranking of results across 11 expert curated research goals generated by AI co-scientist, Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19, Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental, and OpenAI o1, using four different LLM evaluators: OpenAI o3-mini-2025-01-31 (upper left), OpenAI o1-preview-2024-09-12 (upper right), Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental (lower left), and Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 01-21 (lower right). Lower numbers indicate better rankings.
items.capZh: 图8：AI 共同科学家与其他基线的 LLM 偏好排名自动评估。使用四个不同的 LLM 评估者：OpenAI o3-mini-2025-01-31（左上）、OpenAI o1-preview-2024-09-12（右上）、Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental（左下）和 Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 01-21（右下），对 AI 共同科学家、Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 12-19、Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental 和 OpenAI o1 生成的11个专家策划的研究目标的结果进行平均偏好排名。数值越低表示排名越好。

full.sec: 4.4 Safety evaluation of the AI co-scientist using adversarial research goals
full.secZh: 4.4 使用对抗性研究目标对人工智能共同科学家进行安全评估

items.en: The AI co-scientist is designed to empower scientists and accelerate research. However, it’s crucial to ensure the system is designed with robust safety principles, given the potential for misuse. This includes addressing dangerous research goals, dual-use objectives, scenarios where safe goals lead to unsafe hypotheses, misleading claims, and inherent biases. While this topic requires extensive investigation beyond the scope of this work, we employed adversarial testing strategies to conduct a preliminary safety analysis of the system. Specifically, we curated a set of 1200 adversarial examples, ranging in complexity, across 40 biomedical and scientific topics using frontier LLMs. We then evaluated whether the AI co-scientist could robustly reject these research goals. In this preliminary analysis, the system successfully passed all checks. Given the sensitive nature of these adversarial research goals, we will not be publicly releasing the dataset, but it can be made available upon request. Collectively, the benchmark, automated, and expert evaluations presented in this section provide compelling evidence of the system’s strong capabilities.
items.zh: AI 共同科学家旨在赋能科学家并加速研究。然而，鉴于潜在的滥用风险，确保系统设计遵循稳健的安全原则至关重要。这包括处理危险的研究目标、双重用途目标、安全目标导致不安全假设的场景、误导性声明以及固有偏见。虽然该主题需要超出本文范围的广泛研究，但我们采用了对抗性测试策略对系统进行了初步安全分析。具体而言，我们使用前沿 LLM 在40个生物医学和科学主题中策划了1200个对抗性示例，复杂度各异。然后评估 AI 共同科学家能否稳健地拒绝这些研究目标。在此初步分析中，系统成功通过了所有检查。鉴于这些对抗性研究目标的敏感性，我们不会公开发布数据集，但可根据请求提供。总体而言，本节介绍的基准测试、自动评估和专家评估为系统的强大能力提供了令人信服的证据。

full.sec: 4.5 Drug repurposing with the AI co-scientist
full.secZh: 4.5 人工智能共同科学家在药物重定位中的应用

items.en: As previously noted, a rigorous assessment of a system’s ability to generate novel hypotheses and predictions for complex research problems necessitates end-to-end validation through wet-lab experiments. However, due to the challenging, time-consuming, and resource-intensive nature of such endeavors, large-scale experimental validation is infeasible. Instead, we strategically selected diverse yet critical biomedical topics to serve as a strong benchmark for the end-to-end system evaluation. Detailed descriptions of these topics follow. Importantly, all three experimental validations were conducted in collaboration with expert scientists, who provided guidance to the co-scientist and prioritized wet-lab experiments.
items.zh: 如前所述，严格评估系统为复杂研究问题生成新颖假设和预测的能力需要通过湿实验进行端到端验证。然而，由于此类工作具有挑战性、耗时且资源密集，大规模实验验证不可行。相反，我们策略性地选择了多样但关键的生物医学主题，作为系统端到端评估的强基准。以下是对这些主题的详细描述。重要的是，所有三项实验验证均与专家科学家合作进行，他们为共同科学家提供指导并优先安排湿实验。

items.en: We begin the discussion of the end-to-end validation of the AI co-scientist with a drug repurposing application. As introduced earlier, drug repurposing is the process of identifying novel therapeutic indications for existing, approved drugs beyond their original use. This approach can accelerate the discovery of treatments for complex and rare diseases, as repurposed drugs have established safety profiles and are readily available. From a technical standpoint, this is a combinatorial search problem involving a large but finite set of drug-disease pairs as noted in Table˜1.
items.zh: 我们以药物重定位应用开始讨论 AI 共同科学家的端到端验证。如前所述，药物重定位是指为现有已批准药物寻找超出其原始用途的新治疗适应症的过程。这种方法可以加速复杂和罕见疾病治疗方法的发现，因为重定位药物具有已建立的安全性特征且易于获得。从技术角度来看，这是一个组合搜索问题，涉及大量但有限的药物-疾病对，如表1所示。

items.en: Given the co-scientist’s ability to synthesize and integrate information across a vast body of scientific and clinical literature, we hypothesized that drug repurposing would be an ideal test of the system’s capabilities. The system is general-purpose, capable of providing highly detailed and explainable predictions across all known drug-disease pairs. Here, we focused on the computational biology and wet-lab validation of our co-scientist system in the area of drug repurposing for cancer treatment.
items.zh: 鉴于共同科学家能够综合和整合大量科学和临床文献中的信息，我们假设药物重定位将是测试系统能力的理想选择。该系统是通用型的，能够为所有已知的药物-疾病对提供高度详细且可解释的预测。在此，我们专注于共同科学家系统在癌症治疗药物重定位领域的计算生物学和湿实验验证。

items.en: We initially investigated drug-cancer pairs with existing preclinical evidence to validate the plausibility of the hypotheses and predictions generated by the co-scientist (Section˜4.5.1), before expanding to completely novel drug repurposing hypotheses (Section˜4.5.2). The validation of the co-scientist’s predictions was performed using a multi-faceted approach, incorporating computational biology analyses, oncologist expert feedback, and in vitro wet-lab experiments using cancer cell lines.
items.zh: 我们首先研究了具有现有临床前证据的药物-癌症对，以验证共同科学家生成的假设和预测的合理性（第4.5.1节），然后扩展到完全新颖的药物重定位假设（第4.5.2节）。共同科学家预测的验证采用多方面方法，包括计算生物学分析、肿瘤学家专家反馈以及使用癌细胞系的体外湿实验。

items.en: We constrained the AI co-scientist to explore potential repurposing hypotheses from a curated list of 2300 approved drugs across 33 cancer types (Appendix LABEL:sec:eval_data). To achieve this, we modified the prompts used in the Generation and Ranking agent stages to ensure hypotheses generation in this constrained search space; however, the core co-scientist logic remained unchanged. When formulating the research goal for the co-scientist, we explicitly emphasized the following preferences related to drug repurposing:
items.zh: 我们约束 AI 共同科学家从包含33种癌症类型的2300种已批准药物的策划列表中探索潜在的重定位假设（附录LABEL:sec:eval_data）。为此，我们修改了生成和排序智能体阶段使用的提示，以确保在此约束搜索空间中生成假设；然而，核心共同科学家逻辑保持不变。在制定共同科学家的研究目标时，我们明确强调了以下与药物重定位相关的偏好：

items.en: Elucidate the known mechanisms of action and impacted biological pathways of the drug.
items.zh: 阐明药物的已知作用机制和受影响的生物学通路。

items.en: Identify potential diseases or cancer types that could be treatment targets for the drug.
items.zh: 识别可能成为药物治疗靶点的潜在疾病或癌症类型。

items.en: Explain the potential mechanisms by which the drug could exert therapeutic effects.
items.zh: 解释药物可能发挥治疗效果的潜在机制。

items.en: Propose alternative mechanisms of action through which the drug might function in the proposed therapeutic context.
items.zh: 提出药物在拟议治疗背景下可能发挥作用的替代作用机制。

items.en: Identify the diseases / cancers for which the drug is currently approved.
items.zh: 识别药物目前获批的疾病/癌症。

items.en: List the most promising disease / cancer type candidates for repurposing.
items.zh: 列出最有可能用于重定位的疾病/癌症类型候选。

items.en: Discuss prior research and challenges associated with repurposing the drug.
items.zh: 讨论与药物重定位相关的先前研究和挑战。

items.en: For each drug-cancer pair, we also extracted the Cancer Dependency Map (DepMap) probability of dependency (“DepMap score”) [144] (Appendix LABEL:sec:eval_compbio). The DepMap score represents the probability of essentiality for a gene in a given cancer cell lines. We ranked all drug-cancer pairs using a combined metric of the co-scientist review score (ranging from 1 to 5) and the DepMap score (ranging from 0.0 to 1.0). To prioritize the most relevant hypotheses for expert review, we selected only pairs where the co-scientist review score ≥4\geq 4 and the DepMap score ≥0.99\geq 0.99. Note that the DepMap score is primarily meant to function as a sanity check and filter out obviously incorrect candidates but is unlikely to be predictive of efficacy.
items.zh: 对于每个药物-癌症对，我们还提取了癌症依赖性图谱（DepMap）依赖性概率（“DepMap 评分”）[144]（附录LABEL:sec:eval_compbio）。DepMap 评分表示基因在给定癌细胞系中的必需性概率。我们使用共同科学家审查评分（范围1-5）和 DepMap 评分（范围0.0-1.0）的组合指标对所有药物-癌症对进行排名。为了优先选择最相关的假设供专家审查，我们仅选择共同科学家审查评分≥4且 DepMap 评分≥0.99的对。请注意，DepMap 评分主要用于合理性检查并过滤掉明显不正确的候选，但不太可能预测疗效。

items.en: Expert oncologists then reviewed the top-ranked drug-cancer pairs, provided feedback, and selected promising repurposing candidates for in vitro wet-lab validation (Appendix LABEL:sec:eval_wetlab).
items.zh: 专家肿瘤学家随后审查了排名靠前的药物-癌症对，提供反馈，并选择有前景的重定位候选进行体外湿实验验证（附录LABEL:sec:eval_wetlab）。

items.en: To rigorously evaluate whether the co-scientist-generated hypotheses for drug repurposing fulfill the needs of physicians and scientists, we restructured the co-scientist hypotheses into the NIH-style grant proposal Specific Aims Page (examples in Appendix LABEL:fig:aim1_1-LABEL:fig:aim3_2), and asked a team of six expert hematologists & oncologists to evaluate the specific aims.
items.zh: 为了严格评估共同科学家生成的药物重定位假说是否满足医生和科学家的需求，我们将共同科学家的假说重构为NIH风格的资助申请书特定目标页面（示例见附录LABEL:fig:aim1_1-LABEL:fig:aim3_2），并邀请六位血液学与肿瘤学专家团队评估这些特定目标。

items.en: The NIH Specific Aims Page format follows a standard structure, including disease description, unmet need, proposed solutions, and specific aims. This format was selected because it provides a standardized framework that is widely recognized in the research community, allowing for systematic presentation of complex scientific topics in a manner that facilitates rigorous peer review and enables efficient assessment of scientific merit. The specific aims, which outline the overarching goal, hypothesis, and rationale, requires extensive scientific expertise, comprehensive literature analysis, and robust domain knowledge. We generated cancer drug repurposing hypotheses derived from the co-scientist in the format of NIH Specific Aims Page with additional constrained decoding and self-critique stages to ensure format consistency. The resulting format contextualizes proposed repurposing candidates within known mechanisms based on current literature and then extrapolates to a new disease state. An expert oncologist methodically evaluated and excluded hypotheses that were deemed clinically implausible or had limited potential for successful translation, as well as those falling outside the expertise of the assembled specialist evaluators. This initial screening process employed multiple evidence-based criteria including: (1) pharmacological mechanism incompatibility with tumor biology; (2) unfavorable pharmacokinetic profiles for oncological applications; (3) prohibitive toxicity profiles documented in prior clinical use; (4) confounding effects where apparent survival benefits were attributable to improved management of treatment-related morbidity rather than direct anti-neoplastic activity; and (5) insufficient preclinical evidence supporting antitumor efficacy at clinically achievable concentrations. For example, bisphosphonate agents like pamidronate, while associated with improved outcomes in observational studies of patients with bone metastases, were excluded after critical evaluation revealed their benefits stemmed primarily from reduction of skeletal-related events (such as pathological fractures, spinal cord compression, and bone pain requiring radiation) rather than from disease modifying activity of the drug-candidate.
items.zh: NIH特定目标页面格式遵循标准结构，包括疾病描述、未满足需求、拟议解决方案和特定目标。选择该格式是因为它提供了一个研究界广泛认可的标准化框架，能够以促进严格同行评审和高效评估科学价值的方式系统呈现复杂科学主题。特定目标概述了总体目标、假说和基本原理，需要广泛的科学专业知识、全面的文献分析和扎实的领域知识。我们生成了源自共同科学家的癌症药物重定位假说，采用NIH特定目标页面格式，并附加了约束解码和自我批评阶段以确保格式一致性。生成的格式将拟议的重定位候选药物置于基于当前文献的已知机制背景下，然后外推到新的疾病状态。一位专家肿瘤学家系统地评估并排除了那些被认为临床不可行或成功转化潜力有限的假说，以及超出所召集的专科评估专家专业范围的假说。这一初步筛选过程采用了多项基于证据的标准，包括：(1)药理学机制与肿瘤生物学不相容；(2)肿瘤学应用中药代动力学特征不利；(3)既往临床使用中记录的有毒副作用；(4)混杂效应，即明显的生存获益归因于治疗相关发病率的改善而非直接抗肿瘤活性；(5)在临床可达到浓度下支持抗肿瘤疗效的临床前证据不足。例如，双膦酸盐类药物如帕米膦酸，虽然在骨转移患者的观察性研究中与改善结局相关，但在关键评估后发现其益处主要源于减少骨骼相关事件（如病理性骨折、脊髓压迫和需要放疗的骨痛），而非药物候选物的疾病修饰活性。

items.en: Six board-certified hematologists & oncologists from a single institution - including four domain-specific oncologists specializing in gastrointestinal (GI), breast, gynecologic (GYN) and genitourinary (GU) cancers and two general hematologist & oncologists, with an average of eight years of clinical experience - evaluated 78 unique drug repurposing hypotheses presented in the NIH Specific Aims Page format (for specific indication distribution and counts, see Appendix LABEL:sec:specific_aims_count).
items.zh: 六位来自同一机构的委员会认证血液学与肿瘤学专家——包括四位专注于胃肠道（GI）、乳腺、妇科（GYN）和泌尿生殖系统（GU）癌症的专科肿瘤学家和两位普通血液学与肿瘤学专家，平均临床经验八年——评估了以NIH特定目标页面格式呈现的78个独特的药物重定位假说（具体适应症分布和数量见附录LABEL:sec:specific_aims_count）。

items.en: The expert raters evaluated the generated Specific Aims based on a modified NIH grant proposal evaluation rubric, consisting of 15 axes focusing on (1) importance of research (significance and innovation) and (2) approach (rigor and feasibility). The raters indicated their agreement level using a five-point scale: “Strongly Agree”, “Agree”, “Neutral”, “Disagree”, and “Strongly Disagree”. For each axis, we included several questions covering different aspects of the NIH evaluation criteria. The evaluation rubric is further detailed in the Appendix LABEL:sec:specific_aims_rubric. Specifically, we ask raters to focus on evaluating the clinical relevance and potential for clinical translation, and not for translational capacity or the design of clinical trials.
items.zh: 专家评分者根据修改后的NIH资助申请书评估标准评估生成的特定目标，该标准包括15个轴，重点关注(1)研究的重要性（重要性和创新性）和(2)方法（严谨性和可行性）。评分者使用五点量表表示同意程度：“非常同意”、“同意”、“中立”、“不同意”和“非常不同意”。对于每个轴，我们包含了涵盖NIH评估标准不同方面的若干问题。评估标准在附录LABEL:sec:specific_aims_rubric中进一步详述。具体来说，我们要求评分者专注于评估临床相关性和临床转化潜力，而非转化能力或临床试验设计。

items.capEn: Figure 9: Clinical expert evaluation for the co-scientist generated drug repurposing hypotheses in the NIH Specific Aims Page format. Six expert hematologists & oncologists reviewed 78 drug repurposing research proposals, which the co-scientist had formatted as NIH Specific Aims Pages. The evaluation followed an adapted NIH grant proposal evaluation rubric, detailed in Appendix LABEL:sec:specific_aims_rubric. Overall, the oncologists judged the Specific Aims proposals from the AI co-scientist to be of high quality across all axes of the rubric.
items.capZh: 图9：共同科学家生成的药物重定位假说在NIH特定目标页面格式中的临床专家评估。六位血液学与肿瘤学专家审查了78个药物重定位研究提案，这些提案由共同科学家格式化为NIH特定目标页面。评估遵循了改编的NIH资助申请书评估标准，详见附录LABEL:sec:specific_aims_rubric。总体而言，肿瘤学家认为来自AI共同科学家的特定目标提案在标准的所有轴上均具有高质量。

items.en: We observed that expert raters consistently assigned high ratings (“Strongly Agree” or “Agree”) to the Specific Aims proposed by the co-scientist across various evaluation criteria (Figure˜9). Of note, the favorable assessments of co-scientist-generated hypotheses may be partially attributed to expert pre-screening, wherein a clinician eliminated non-viable candidates prior to expert evaluation. Three examples of generated Specific Aims and their respective expert review ratings are detailed in Appendix LABEL:fig:aim1_1-LABEL:fig:aim3_2.
items.zh: 我们观察到专家评分者一致对共同科学家提出的特定目标在各种评估标准上给予高分（“非常同意”或“同意”）（图9）。值得注意的是，对共同科学家生成假说的有利评估可能部分归因于专家预筛选，其中一位临床医生在专家评估前排除了不可行的候选方案。生成的特定目标及其各自专家评审评分的三个示例详见附录LABEL:fig:aim1_1-LABEL:fig:aim3_2。

items.en: The generated Specific Aims were assessed by clinical hematologists & oncologists from a single-center, which might bias the interpretation of the evaluation results, as it may introduce institutional perspectives shaped by local practice patterns, clinical experiences, and research frameworks unique to that setting. While some Specific Aims may be supported by preclinical data, it is important to note that none of the proposed drug candidates have undergone randomized phase III clinical trials necessary to establish efficacy and secure regulatory approval for repurposing to a new indication.
items.zh: 生成的特定目标由来自单一中心的临床血液学与肿瘤学专家评估，这可能会使评估结果的解释产生偏差，因为它可能引入由该机构特有的局部实践模式、临床经验和研究框架所形成的机构视角。虽然某些特定目标可能有临床前数据支持，但重要的是要注意，所提出的药物候选物均未经过必要的随机III期临床试验，以建立疗效并获得监管批准用于新适应症的重定位。

items.en: Building upon the positive feedback from clinical experts, we conducted in vitro wet-lab validation experiments for drug repurposing hypotheses generated by the co-scientist for acute myeloid leukemia (AML). AML is an aggressive and relatively rare blood cancer characterized by the rapid proliferation of abnormal white blood cells (myeloblasts) in the bone marrow, which displaces healthy blood cells. We focused on this indication due to its aggressive nature and the limited availability of effective therapeutic interventions [73].
items.zh: 基于临床专家的积极反馈，我们对共同科学家为急性髓系白血病（AML）生成的药物重定位假说进行了体外湿实验验证。AML是一种侵袭性且相对罕见的血癌，其特征是骨髓中异常白细胞（髓母细胞）快速增殖，取代健康血细胞。我们关注这一适应症是因为其侵袭性以及有效治疗干预的有限可用性[73]。

items.en: The cell-line based experiments conducted here serve as an initial biological validation step for co-scientist hypotheses, with intentionally straightforward methodology following established protocols. The simplicity in experimental design is purposeful; our focus is on evaluating the merit of AI co-scientist generated hypotheses rather than developing novel laboratory techniques. Positive results from these experiments should be interpreted as preliminary evidence warranting further investigation through comprehensive pre-clinical studies (e.g., in vivo models) and potentially clinical evaluation.
items.zh: 这里进行的基于细胞系的实验作为共同科学家假说的初步生物学验证步骤，采用有意简化的方法学，遵循既定方案。实验设计的简单性是有目的的；我们的重点是评估AI共同科学家生成假说的价值，而非开发新的实验室技术。这些实验的阳性结果应解释为初步证据，值得通过全面的临床前研究（例如体内模型）和潜在的临床评估进行进一步研究。

items.en: It is important to emphasize that these wet-lab experiments function as a viability checkpoint in the drug repurposing pipeline - not as a replacement for the rigorous pre-clinical and clinical assessment typically required for therapeutic validation. They provide an efficient biological reality check that helps bridge the gap between computational predictions and potential clinical applications, allowing us to rapidly triage AI-generated hypotheses before committing to more resource-intensive validation studies necessary for clinical translation.
items.zh: 需要强调的是，这些湿实验在药物重定位流程中充当可行性检查点——而非替代治疗验证通常所需的严格临床前和临床评估。它们提供了有效的生物学现实检验，有助于弥合计算预测与潜在临床应用之间的差距，使我们能够在投入更资源密集的验证研究（临床转化所需）之前快速筛选AI生成的假说。

items.capEn: Figure 10: Dose response curve of the expert selected repurposing drugs with existing evidence. Binimetinib, Pacritinib, and Cerivastatin inhibit MOLM-13 cell viability. X-axis is the drug concentration (nM), and Y-axis is normalized cell viability (arbitrary unit). Lower cell viability indicates that the selected drug has a stronger inhibition on AML cells.
items.capZh: 图10：专家选择的具有现有证据的重定位药物的剂量反应曲线。Binimetinib、Pacritinib和Cerivastatin抑制MOLM-13细胞活力。X轴为药物浓度（nM），Y轴为归一化细胞活力（任意单位）。较低的细胞活力表明所选药物对AML细胞具有更强的抑制作用。

items.en: The candidate selection for wet-lab experiments was performed with meticulous expert oversight. Thirty top-ranked drug candidates hypotheses were shared with expert oncologists (an example detailed co-scientist output is provided in LABEL:sec:drug_output). The experts evaluated the hypotheses, selecting drug candidates based on their potential to modulate key molecular signaling pathways associated with disease progression and resistance. Note that we did not preclude any hypotheses based on whether single-agent therapy has been studied before, or if the phase III has been approved. The primary selection criterion favored compounds with multi-pathway activity, specifically those influencing dysregulated inflammatory signaling, metabolic reprogramming, and aberrant cell proliferation. Emerging research indicates that these shared biological processes play a critical role in relapse and treatment resistance [74]. Candidates were also chosen based on preclinical mechanistic insights and their relevance to AML biology, including their hypothesized effects on leukemic cell survival, microenvironment interactions, and resistance mechanisms.
items.zh: 湿实验的候选药物选择是在细致的专家监督下进行的。三十个排名靠前的药物候选假说与专家肿瘤学家共享（共同科学家输出的详细示例见LABEL:sec:drug_output）。专家评估了这些假说，根据候选药物调节与疾病进展和耐药性相关的关键分子信号通路的潜力进行选择。注意，我们并未基于单药治疗是否曾被研究或III期试验是否已获批而排除任何假说。主要选择标准倾向于具有多通路活性的化合物，特别是那些影响失调的炎症信号、代谢重编程和异常细胞增殖的化合物。新兴研究表明，这些共享的生物学过程在复发和治疗耐药中起关键作用[74]。候选药物还根据临床前机制见解及其与AML生物学的相关性进行选择，包括它们对白血病细胞存活、微环境相互作用和耐药机制的假设效应。

items.en: Based on potential mechanisms of action, five drug repurposing candidates—Binimetinib, Pacritinib, Cerivastatin, Pravastatin, and Dimethyl fumarate (DMF)—were selected for further wet-lab validation in AML.
items.zh: 基于潜在作用机制，选择了五种药物重定位候选物——Binimetinib、Pacritinib、Cerivastatin、Pravastatin和富马酸二甲酯（DMF）——用于AML的进一步湿实验验证。

items.en: Briefly, Binimetinib is an inhibitor of MEK1 and MEK2, key kinases in the RAS–RAF–MEK–ERK signaling pathway. By inhibiting MEK1/2, binimetinib prevents the activation of ERK (extracellular signal-regulated kinase), thereby blocking downstream signaling that promotes cell proliferation and survival [75]. Although RAS mutations typically emerge as late events in AML pathogenesis, Binimetinib was included to investigate its potential to modulate RAS-MEK-ERK signaling in treatment-naive AML, where baseline expression levels of this pathway can influence sensitivity to conventional chemotherapeutic agents [76].
items.zh: 简而言之，Binimetinib是MEK1和MEK2的抑制剂，MEK1和MEK2是RAS–RAF–MEK–ERK信号通路中的关键激酶。通过抑制MEK1/2，Binimetinib阻止ERK（细胞外信号调节激酶）的激活，从而阻断促进细胞增殖和存活的下游信号[75]。尽管RAS突变通常作为AML发病机制中的晚期事件出现，但Binimetinib被纳入以研究其在初治AML中调节RAS-MEK-ERK信号通路的潜力，其中该通路的基线表达水平可能影响对常规化疗药物的敏感性[76]。

items.en: Pacritinib is an oral tyrosine kinase inhibitor that selectively targets JAK2 and FLT3 kinases [77]. By blocking JAK2’s kinase activity, pacritinib suppresses the overactive JAK-STAT signaling that drives pathogenic cell proliferation and cytokine production in diseases such as myelofibrosis. It was selected for repurposing due to the dual inhibition of growth signaling pathways: the JAK2/STAT pathway, critical in hematopoietic cell growth and inflammatory signaling, and FLT3-driven proliferative signaling that regulates leukemic cell survival and also facilitates the development of escape pathways to targeted therapies [78].
items.zh: Pacritinib是一种口服酪氨酸激酶抑制剂，选择性靶向JAK2和FLT3激酶[77]。通过阻断JAK2的激酶活性，Pacritinib抑制了在骨髓纤维化等疾病中驱动致病性细胞增殖和细胞因子产生的过度活跃的JAK-STAT信号。它被选择用于重定位是因为对生长信号通路的双重抑制：JAK2/STAT通路（在造血细胞生长和炎症信号中至关重要）和FLT3驱动的增殖信号（调节白血病细胞存活并促进靶向治疗逃逸途径的发展）[78]。

items.en: Dimethyl fumarate (DMF) is an immunomodulatory drug that activates the Nrf2 (nuclear factor erythroid 2–related factor 2) pathway via covalent modification of the cysteine residues on Keap1, the cytosolic protein that normally binds Nrf2 and targets it for degradation. By oxidizing or alkylating the thiol groups of Keap1, DMF destabilizes the Keap1–Nrf2 complex, allowing Nrf2 to escape ubiquitination and translocate into the nucleus. In parallel, DMF also inhibits NFκ\kappaB mediated transcription and was chosen for repurposing due to clinically relevant activity of NFκ\kappaB in AML [79, 80].
items.zh: 富马酸二甲酯（DMF）是一种免疫调节药物，通过共价修饰Keap1上的半胱氨酸残基激活Nrf2（核因子红细胞2相关因子2）通路，Keap1是通常结合Nrf2并靶向其降解的胞质蛋白。通过氧化或烷基化Keap1的硫醇基团，DMF使Keap1–Nrf2复合物不稳定，使Nrf2逃脱泛素化并转位到细胞核。同时，DMF还抑制NFκB介导的转录，并因NFκB在AML中的临床相关活性而被选择用于重定位[79, 80]。

items.en: Finally, the statins (Cerivastatin and Pravastatin) were selected for their potential to induce metabolic reprogramming and directly modulate vesicular transport mechanisms in rapidly proliferating cells [81].
items.zh: 最后，他汀类药物（Cerivastatin和Pravastatin）因其在快速增殖细胞中诱导代谢重编程和直接调节囊泡运输机制的潜力而被选中[81]。

items.en: Of the five drugs tested, Binimetinib, Pacritinib, and Cerivastatin demonstrated inhibition of cell viability (Figure˜10). Notably, Binimetinib, which is already approved for the treatment of metastatic melanoma, exhibited an IC50 as low as 7 nM in AML cell lines (Figure˜10 and Appendix LABEL:fig:fig_wetlab_appendix). This result shows that the drugs proposed by the co-scientist hold promise as clinically viable drug repurposing candidates. Moreover, this opens the possibility that the co-scientist may be able to expand its hypotheses to novel drug repurposing candidates.
items.zh: 在测试的五种药物中，Binimetinib、Pacritinib和Cerivastatin显示出对细胞活力的抑制（图10）。值得注意的是，已获批用于治疗转移性黑色素瘤的Binimetinib在AML细胞系中表现出低至7 nM的IC50（图10和附录LABEL:fig:fig_wetlab_appendix）。这一结果表明，共同科学家提出的药物作为临床可行的药物重定位候选物具有前景。此外，这开启了共同科学家可能将其假说扩展到新型药物重定位候选物的可能性。

items.en: We aimed to demonstrate the co-scientist’s capacity to autonomously propose novel drug repurposing candidates without oversight. Towards this, the system was directed to generate a ranked list of repurposing candidates for AML, including drugs that were not previously repurposed for the target indication and without any prior preclinical evidence. Specifically, we tasked the co-scientist with generating potential novel drug repurposing hypotheses for AML without explicitly relying on additional external inputs, such as the DepMap scores or human expert feedback. We then determined if these novel candidates suggested by co-scientist could be validated in the laboratory, and may therefore have the potential to be repurposed for AML.
items.zh: 我们旨在展示共同科学家在没有监督的情况下自主提出新型药物重定位候选物的能力。为此，系统被指示生成AML的重定位候选物排名列表，包括之前未针对目标适应症重定位且没有任何临床前证据的药物。具体来说，我们要求共同科学家生成AML的潜在新型药物重定位假说，而不明确依赖额外的外部输入，如DepMap评分或人类专家反馈。然后，我们确定共同科学家建议的这些新型候选物是否可以在实验室中得到验证，并因此可能具有重定位用于AML的潜力。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Dose response curve of novel drug repurposing candidate for AML. KIRA6 activity inhibiting KG-1, MOLM-13, and HL-60 cell viability all in nM range of drug concentration. X-axis is the drug concentration (nM), and Y-axis is normalized cell viability (arbitrary unit). Lower cell viability indicates that the selected drug has a stronger inhibition on AML cells.
items.capZh: 图11：AML新型药物重定位候选物的剂量反应曲线。KIRA6活性抑制KG-1、MOLM-13和HL-60细胞活力，均在nM药物浓度范围内。X轴为药物浓度（nM），Y轴为归一化细胞活力（任意单位）。较低的细胞活力表明所选药物对AML细胞具有更强的抑制作用。

items.en: For in vitro laboratory validation of novel repurposing drugs, the domain experts selected the top 3 candidates from the ranked list, using the criteria that no prior preclinical or clinical data existed with respect to their use to treat AML - Nanvuranlat, KIRA6, and Leflunomide.
items.zh: 对于新型药物重定位的体外实验室验证，领域专家从排名列表中选出了前三名候选药物，筛选标准是这些药物在治疗AML方面此前没有临床前或临床数据——即Nanvuranlat、KIRA6和来氟米特。

items.en: To demonstrate the hypothesis and the rationale given by the AI co-scientist for these drug repurposing candidates, the detailed AI co-scientist output, including the novelty review, is provided in LABEL:sec:drug_output for KIRA6. As can be the seen, the system identifies that targeting IRE1α\alpha in the context of AML has been explored before but not with the specific drug, KIRA6. The system suggests an overall moderate level of novelty for the hypothesis.
items.zh: 为了展示AI共同科学家针对这些药物重定位候选药物提出的假设和理由，LABEL:sec:drug_output中提供了KIRA6的详细AI共同科学家输出，包括新颖性审查。可以看出，系统识别出在AML背景下靶向IRE1α\alpha此前已被探索过，但未使用特定药物KIRA6。系统认为该假设的新颖性总体处于中等水平。

items.en: Of the three drugs tested, treatment with the IRE1α\alpha inhibitor KIRA6 showed inhibition of cell viability in three different AML cell lines, KG-1, MOLM-13, and HL-60 cells (Figure˜11). IC50s of KIRA6 were all in nM range, but significantly more effective in KG-1 cells, which had an IC50 of 13 nM, compared to MOLM-13 and HL60 cells, which had IC50s of 517 nM and 817 nM, respectively. Thus, co-scientist was able to suggest a novel candidate for drug repurposing for AML, beyond those that may have been selected through other existing approaches and expert sources. This suggests that the co-scientist system may therefore be capable of generating new, promising hypotheses for researchers to investigate, that may in the future bring new treatments to patients for complex and challenging diseases such as AML.
items.zh: 在测试的三种药物中，使用IRE1α\alpha抑制剂KIRA6处理三种不同的AML细胞系（KG-1、MOLM-13和HL-60细胞）后，细胞活力受到抑制（图˜11）。KIRA6的IC50均在nM范围内，但在KG-1细胞中效果显著更强，IC50为13 nM，而MOLM-13和HL60细胞的IC50分别为517 nM和817 nM。因此，共同科学家能够为AML的药物重定位提出一个新颖的候选药物，超越了通过其他现有方法和专家来源可能选择的药物。这表明共同科学家系统可能能够为研究人员生成新的、有前景的假设，未来可能为AML等复杂且具有挑战性的疾病患者带来新的治疗方法。

items.en: Translating these insights from co-scientist’s drug repurposing hypotheses into clinical practice will be highly challenging, as the complexity of a disease model, patient heterogeneity, and disease variability cannot be fully captured in such limited in vitro experiments. Even if a hypothesis generated by co-scientist is well-reviewed by oncologists and supported by preclinical rationale and strong in vitro experiments, this does not guarantee in vivo efficacy or clinical success. Factors such as drug bioavailability, pharmacokinetics, off-target effects, and patient selection criteria can all impact onward clinical trial outcomes. Moreover, in case of hematological malignancies, the tumor microenvironment and systemic interactions may introduce unforeseen resistance mechanisms, further complicating translation from hypothesis to clinical benefit.
items.zh: 将这些来自共同科学家药物重定位假设的见解转化为临床实践将极具挑战性，因为疾病模型的复杂性、患者异质性和疾病变异性无法在如此有限的体外实验中完全捕捉。即使共同科学家生成的假设经过肿瘤学家的充分审查，并得到临床前理论和强有力的体外实验支持，也不能保证体内疗效或临床成功。药物生物利用度、药代动力学、脱靶效应和患者选择标准等因素都可能影响后续临床试验的结果。此外，在血液恶性肿瘤的情况下，肿瘤微环境和系统性相互作用可能引入不可预见的耐药机制，进一步使从假设到临床获益的转化复杂化。

full.sec: 4.6 The AI co-scientist uncovers novel therapeutic targets for liver fibrosis
full.secZh: 4.6 人工智能共同科学家发现肝纤维化的新治疗靶点

items.en: Liver fibrosis is a severe disease that can progress to liver failure and hepatocellular carcinoma, which has few treatment options due to the limitations of available animal and in vitro models. However, a recently developed method for producing human hepatic organoids coupled with a live cell imaging system for liver fibrosis provides a new avenue for identification of new treatments for liver fibrosis [82, 83, 84]. The AI co-scientist was asked to produce experimentally testable hypotheses concerning the role of epigenetic alterations in liver fibrosis (“A Novel Hypothesis Regarding Myofibroblast Generation in Liver Fibrosis”); and to identify drugs targeting epigenetic modifiers that could be used for treatment of liver fibrosis.
items.zh: 肝纤维化是一种严重的疾病，可进展为肝衰竭和肝细胞癌，由于现有动物和体外模型的局限性，治疗选择很少。然而，最近开发的一种利用人肝类器官结合活细胞成像系统研究肝纤维化的方法，为识别肝纤维化的新治疗方法提供了新途径[82, 83, 84]。AI共同科学家被要求生成关于表观遗传改变在肝纤维化中作用的可实验检验的假设（“关于肝纤维化中肌成纤维细胞生成的新假设”）；并识别可用于治疗肝纤维化的靶向表观遗传修饰剂的药物。

items.capEn: Figure 12: The co-scientist discovers the novel treatment targets for liver fibrosis. Four drugs (Suggested 1-4) based on three epigenetic targets suggested by the co-scientist decrease the fold change of the fibroblast activity. The experiments were conducted in the human hepatic organoid system, the fibroblast activity was measured by the percentage fold change of fluorescence labelled on the myofibroblast. ‘Untreated’ indicates the normal control, the fibrosis inducer is the fibrogenic agent TGF-β\beta, the fibrosis inhibitor indicates the fibrogenic stimulated myofibroblast treated with the fibrogenic agent inhibitor (i.e., TGF-β\beta inhibitor), and four suggested drugs indicate the fibrogenic stimulated myofibroblast treated with each of the four drugs based on three AI co-scientist suggested epigenetic targets. The red line in each box is the median fold change of the group. The pp-value indicates the statistical significance between the fibrosis inducer group and the given group. These results will be further detailed in an upcoming report.
items.capZh: 图12：共同科学家发现肝纤维化的新治疗靶点。基于共同科学家建议的三个表观遗传靶点的四种药物（建议1-4）降低了成纤维细胞活性的倍数变化。实验在人肝类器官系统中进行，成纤维细胞活性通过肌成纤维细胞上荧光标记的百分比倍数变化测量。“未处理”表示正常对照，纤维化诱导剂是纤维化因子TGF-β\beta，纤维化抑制剂表示用纤维化因子抑制剂（即TGF-β\beta抑制剂）处理的纤维化刺激的肌成纤维细胞，四种建议药物表示用基于AI共同科学家建议的三个表观遗传靶点的四种药物分别处理的纤维化刺激的肌成纤维细胞。每个方框中的红线是该组的中位数倍数变化。p值表示纤维化诱导剂组与给定组之间的统计显著性。这些结果将在即将发布的报告中进一步详述。

items.en: The experts selected three (from fifteen) top-ranked co-scientist generated research hypotheses with a comprehensive research proposal (i.e., experimental design, evaluation methodology, and anticipated results) for exploring the role of epigenetic modifications in liver fibrosis. The co-scientist identified three novel epigenetic modifiers with supporting preclinical evidence that could be targeted by existing agents and provide new treatments for liver fibrosis. Drugs targeting two of the three epigenetic modifiers exhibited significant anti-fibrotic activity in hepatic organoids without causing cellular toxicity (Figure˜12). Since one of them is an FDA-approved drug for another indication, this creates an opportunity to re-purpose a drug for treatment of liver fibrosis. These results will be detailed in an upcoming technical report.
items.zh: 专家从共同科学家生成的十五个研究假设中选出了三个排名靠前的假设，并附有全面的研究方案（即实验设计、评估方法和预期结果），用于探索表观遗传修饰在肝纤维化中的作用。共同科学家识别出三个新颖的表观遗传修饰剂，并附有支持性临床前证据，这些修饰剂可通过现有药物靶向，为肝纤维化提供新治疗方法。靶向其中两个表观遗传修饰剂的药物在肝类器官中表现出显著抗纤维化活性，且未引起细胞毒性（图˜12）。由于其中一种药物是FDA批准的用于其他适应症的药物，这为将药物重定位用于治疗肝纤维化创造了机会。这些结果将在即将发布的技术报告中详述。

full.sec: 4.7 The AI co-scientist recapitulates a breakthrough in antimicrobial resistance
full.secZh: 4.7 人工智能共同科学家重现抗微生物耐药性的突破

items.en: Understanding the mechanisms of antibiotic resistance is crucial for researchers to develop effective treatments against infectious diseases. We focused on capsid-forming phage-inducible chromosomal islands (cf-PICIs), which play a pivotal role in antibiotic resistance. These mobile genetic elements, unlike typical phages and other PICIs, possess a remarkable ability to transfer between diverse bacterial species, carrying with them virulence and antibiotic resistance genes. We sought to understand the evolutionary rationale behind the existence of cf-PICIs across multiple bacterial species in order to develop solutions to combat antimicrobial resistance.
items.zh: 理解抗生素耐药性机制对于研究人员开发针对传染病的有效治疗方法至关重要。我们聚焦于形成衣壳的噬菌体诱导染色体岛（cf-PICI），它们在抗生素耐药性中发挥关键作用。这些可移动遗传元件与典型噬菌体和其他PICI不同，具有在不同细菌物种之间转移的显著能力，并携带毒力和抗生素耐药性基因。我们试图理解cf-PICI在多个细菌物种中存在的进化原理，以便制定对抗抗菌药物耐药性的解决方案。

items.en: The primary objective was to leverage the AI co-scientist to generate a research proposal aimed at elucidating the molecular mechanisms of bacterial evolution underlying the broad host range of cf-PICIs and developing strategies to curb the spread of antibiotic resistance. We specifically focused on the observation that identical cf-PICIs, such as PICIEc1 and PICIKp1, are found in clinically relevant bacterial species, including WHO priority pathogens like Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae.
items.zh: 主要目标是利用AI共同科学家生成一个研究方案，旨在阐明cf-PICI广泛宿主范围背后的细菌进化分子机制，并制定遏制抗生素耐药性传播的策略。我们特别关注一个观察结果：相同的cf-PICI，如PICIEc1和PICIKp1，存在于临床相关的细菌物种中，包括WHO优先病原体如大肠杆菌和肺炎克雷伯菌。

items.en: In a co-timed study [85] currently undergoing the peer-review process at an established journal in the field, genomic and experimental studies revealed a novel mechanism explaining how identical cf-PICIs can be found in different bacterial species. Knowing the answer to that question (but without it yet being available in the public domain), we investigated whether the co-scientist could independently discover the same, or similar, research hypotheses. We provided the co-scientist with a single-page document containing general information, including a brief background on phage satellites and two relevant research articles. The first paper described the original discovery of cf-PICIs [86], and the second paper introduced a computational technique for identifying phage satellites in bacterial genomes [87]. We then challenged co-scientist to address the question of why cf-PICIs, but not other types of PICIs or satellites, are readily found across diverse bacterial species, and what mechanism underlies this phenomenon.
items.zh: 在一项正在进行同行评审的联合研究[85]中，基因组和实验研究揭示了一种新机制，解释了相同的cf-PICI如何存在于不同的细菌物种中。在已知该问题答案（但尚未公开）的情况下，我们研究了共同科学家是否能够独立发现相同或相似的研究假设。我们向共同科学家提供了一份单页文档，包含一般信息，包括关于噬菌体卫星的背景简介和两篇相关研究文章。第一篇论文描述了cf-PICI的原始发现[86]，第二篇论文介绍了一种用于识别细菌基因组中噬菌体卫星的计算技术[87]。然后，我们要求共同科学家回答为什么cf-PICI（而非其他类型的PICI或卫星）容易在不同细菌物种中发现，以及这种现象背后的机制是什么。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Timeline of the conventional experimental validation and the AI co-scientist-assisted hypothesis development for capsid-forming phage-inducible chromosomal islands (cf-PICIs), key to antibiotic resistance (AMR). Blue box: Scientist inputs. Red box: The AI co-scientist system. Red text: wet lab experimental setting. Blue text: research hypothesis generation.
items.capZh: 图13：传统实验验证与AI共同科学家辅助假设开发的时间线，针对形成衣壳的噬菌体诱导染色体岛（cf-PICI），这是抗生素耐药性（AMR）的关键。蓝色框：科学家输入。红色框：AI共同科学家系统。红色文字：湿实验室实验设置。蓝色文字：研究假设生成。

items.en: The co-scientist independently and accurately proposed a groundbreaking hypothesis—that cf-PICIs elements interact with diverse phage tails to expand their host range—as its top-ranked suggestion [21]. This finding was experimentally validated in the independent research study, which was unknown to the co-scientist during hypothesis generation [20]. Its worth noting that while the co-scientist generated this hypothesis in just two days, it was building on decades of research and had access to all prior open access literature on this topic.
items.zh: 共同科学家独立且准确地提出了一个突破性假设——cf-PICI元件与多种噬菌体尾部相互作用以扩展其宿主范围——作为其排名最高的建议[21]。这一发现在一项独立研究（共同科学家在假设生成时未知）中得到了实验验证[20]。值得注意的是，尽管共同科学家仅用两天就生成了这一假设，但它建立在数十年的研究基础上，并能够访问该主题的所有先前开放获取文献。

items.en: (Figure˜13). Specifically, the co-scientist suggested the following research topics for the given research goal regarding cf-PICIs:
items.zh: （图˜13）。具体来说，共同科学家针对cf-PICI的给定研究目标提出了以下研究主题：

items.en: Capsid-tail interactions. Investigate the interactions between cf-PICI capsids and a broad range of helper phage tails. This topic aligns precisely with the unpublished manuscript’s primary finding: that cf-PICIs interact with tails from different phages to expand their host range, a process mediated by cf-PICI-encoded adaptor and connector proteins.
items.zh: 衣壳-尾部相互作用。研究cf-PICI衣壳与广泛辅助噬菌体尾部之间的相互作用。该主题与未发表手稿的主要发现完全一致：cf-PICI通过与不同噬菌体的尾部相互作用来扩展其宿主范围，这一过程由cf-PICI编码的适配器和连接蛋白介导。

items.en: Integration mechanisms. Examine the mechanisms by which cf-PICIs integrate into the genomes of diverse bacterial species.
items.zh: 整合机制。检查cf-PICI整合到不同细菌物种基因组中的机制。

items.en: Entry mechanisms. Explore alternative cf-PICI entry mechanisms beyond traditional phage receptor recognition.
items.zh: 进入机制。探索超越传统噬菌体受体识别的替代cf-PICI进入机制。

items.en: Helper phage and environmental factors. Investigate the role of helper phages and broader ecological factors in cf-PICI transfer.
items.zh: 辅助噬菌体和环境因素。研究辅助噬菌体和更广泛的生态因素在cf-PICI转移中的作用。

items.en: Alternative transfer and stabilization mechanisms. Explore other potential transfer mechanisms, such as conjugation, extracellular vesicles, and unique stabilization strategies, that might contribute to cf-PICI’s broad host range.
items.zh: 替代转移和稳定机制。探索其他可能的转移机制，如接合、细胞外囊泡和独特的稳定策略，这些可能有助于cf-PICI的广泛宿主范围。

items.en: The convergence of the conventional and AI co-scientist approaches on the same novel finding underscores the potential of the co-scientist to augment, complement and accelerate scientific endeavors (Figure˜13). Further results and comprehensive details are available in the companion report [21].
items.zh: 传统方法与AI共同科学家方法在同一新颖发现上的趋同，突显了共同科学家增强、补充和加速科学努力的潜力（图˜13）。进一步的结果和全面细节可在配套报告[21]中找到。

full.secZh: 5 局限性

items.en: We are encouraged by the early promise of the AI co-scientist evaluations, which highlight its potential to augment scientific research. However, the system has several limitations. Responsible innovation necessitates thoughtful consideration of these alongside the potential impacts to researchers and scientific research.
items.zh: 我们对AI共同科学家评估的早期前景感到鼓舞，这突显了其增强科学研究的潜力。然而，该系统存在若干局限性。负责任的创新需要深思熟虑这些局限性以及对研究人员和科学研究的潜在影响。

items.en: The reviews undertaken by the AI co-scientist system may miss critical prior works due to reliance on open-access literature. In the presented work, the AI co-scientist does not access the entire published literature due to compliance with license or access restrictions where applicable. The system may also omit consideration of prior work on occasions where it has incorrectly reasoned that the work is not relevant.
items.zh: AI 共同科学家系统进行的综述可能因依赖开放获取文献而遗漏重要的先前工作。在所呈现的工作中，由于遵守适用的许可或访问限制，AI 共同科学家无法访问整个已发表文献。该系统还可能因错误判断某工作不相关而忽略对其的考虑。

items.en: The AI co-scientist system’s use of only open published literature means it likely has limited access to negative experimental results or records of failed experiments. It is known that such data may be more rarely published than positive results, yet experienced scientists working in the field may nonetheless possess and utilize this knowledge to prioritize research [88]. Strategies to overcome this phenomenon might further improve the performance of the co-scientist as a tool for scientific discovery.
items.zh: AI 共同科学家系统仅使用公开的已发表文献，这意味着它可能难以获取阴性实验结果或失败实验的记录。已知此类数据比阳性结果更少发表，但领域内经验丰富的科学家可能仍拥有并利用这些知识来优先安排研究[88]。克服这一现象的策略可能进一步提升共同科学家作为科学发现工具的性能。

items.en: Some of the most interesting data in scientific publications is not written in text but may be encoded visually in figures and charts. However, even state-of-the-art frontier models may not comprehensively utilize such data with optimal reasoning [89] and the AI co-scientist system is unlikely to be an exception. Stronger benchmarks and evaluations are necessary to improve these capabilities. We have also not evaluated the ability of our system to reason over and integrate information from domain-specific biomedical multimodal datasets (such as large multi-omics datasets) and knowledge graphs. More work is needed to integrate the AI co-scientist system with specialized scientific tools, AI models and databases, and evaluate the ability to utilize them effectively.
items.zh: 科学出版物中最有趣的一些数据并非以文本形式呈现，而是以图表形式编码。然而，即使是最先进的前沿模型也可能无法以最优推理全面利用此类数据[89]，AI 共同科学家系统也不例外。需要更强的基准和评估来提升这些能力。我们尚未评估系统推理和整合领域特定生物医学多模态数据集（如大型多组学数据集）和知识图谱的能力。需要更多工作将 AI 共同科学家系统与专业科学工具、AI 模型和数据库集成，并评估其有效利用这些资源的能力。

items.en: LLM limitations include imperfect factuality and hallucinations, which may be propagated in the co-scientist system. The system’s reliance on existing LLMs and web-search, while providing immediate access to broad knowledge, may propagate errors of factuality, biases or limitations present in those resources.
items.zh: LLM 的局限性包括不完美的事实性和幻觉，这些可能在共同科学家系统中传播。系统对现有 LLM 和网络搜索的依赖，虽然提供了对广泛知识的即时访问，但可能传播这些资源中存在的事实性错误、偏见或局限性。

items.en: While the current AI co-scientist evaluation includes AI auto-ratings, expert reviews and targeted in vitro validations, the evaluation of system performance remains preliminary. A comprehensive, systematic evaluation across diverse biomedical and scientific disciplines is necessary to determine the generalizability of co-scientist. Furthermore, the system requires continued improvement to produce outputs that meet the rigor and detail of high-quality publications. Furthermore, the Elo rating implemented to help the system self-improve for hypothesis generation is a limited auto-evaluation metric. Continued investigation into alternative, more objective, less intrinsically-favored, evaluation metrics that better represent perspectives and preferences from expert scientists could strengthen future work.
items.zh: 虽然当前的 AI 共同科学家评估包括 AI 自动评分、专家评审和靶向体外验证，但系统性能的评估仍是初步的。需要在不同生物医学和科学学科中进行全面、系统的评估，以确定共同科学家的泛化能力。此外，系统需要持续改进，以产生符合高质量出版物严谨性和细节的输出。此外，为帮助系统自我改进假设生成而实施的 Elo 评分是一种有限的自动评估指标。继续研究更客观、更少内在偏好、更能代表专家科学家观点和偏好的替代评估指标，将有助于加强未来工作。

items.en: At present, the AI co-scientist focuses on identifying potential therapeutic targets and mechanisms, but many not be addressing the complexities of drug delivery systems. Pharmaceutical factors such as tissue-specific targeting, formulation requirements, and delivery efficiency—while critical for clinical translation—remain beyond the scope of the present system.
items.zh: 目前，AI 共同科学家专注于识别潜在的治疗靶点和机制，但可能未解决药物递送系统的复杂性。药物因素如组织特异性靶向、制剂要求和递送效率——尽管对临床转化至关重要——仍超出当前系统的范围。

items.en: The AI co-scientist is currently also not designed to generate comprehensive clinical trial designs or to fully account for factors such as drug bioavailability, pharmacokinetics, and any complex drug interactions when applied for drug repurposing or discovery. These aspects require much deeper understanding, extensive expertise, and appropriate data beyond the scope of the current system. A dedicated translational scientific team is needed for onward clinical translation of the predictions. These limitations also highlights the need for continued development and integration of the system with more tools, such as specialized AI models and real-time databases.
items.zh: AI 共同科学家目前也并非设计用于生成全面的临床试验设计，或在药物重定位或发现中充分考虑药物生物利用度、药代动力学和任何复杂的药物相互作用等因素。这些方面需要更深入的理解、广泛的专业知识和适当的数据，超出了当前系统的范围。需要专门的转化科学团队来推进预测的临床转化。这些局限性也凸显了系统持续开发并与更多工具（如专业 AI 模型和实时数据库）集成的必要性。

full.secZh: 6 安全与伦理影响

items.en: While AI systems such as the co-scientist offers the potential to accelerate scientific discovery, it also poses significant safety and ethical challenges, distinct from its impact on the scientific method itself. Safety risks center on the dual-use and the possibility that scientific breakthroughs could be exploited for harmful purposes. Ethical risks, conversely, involve research that contradicts established ethical norms and conventions within specific scientific disciplines. We review these distinct risk categories, emphasizing that further research is crucial to fully understand and mitigate them.
items.zh: 虽然像共同科学家这样的 AI 系统有潜力加速科学发现，但它也带来了重大的安全和伦理挑战，这与其对科学方法本身的影响不同。安全风险集中于双重用途以及科学突破可能被用于有害目的的可能性。相反，伦理风险涉及与特定科学学科中既定伦理规范和惯例相矛盾的研究。我们回顾了这些不同的风险类别，强调进一步研究对于充分理解和缓解它们至关重要。

items.en: Research ethics is a central aspect of scientific endeavor and a prominent research field in its own right [90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95]. A key focus is directing research towards positive societal impact, although questions remain about potentially dual-use knowledge [96, 97, 98, 99, 100].
items.zh: 研究伦理是科学事业的核心方面，本身也是一个重要的研究领域[90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95]。一个关键焦点是将研究导向积极的社会影响，尽管关于潜在双重用途知识的问题仍然存在[96, 97, 98, 99, 100]。

items.en: Core ethics principles are being complemented by emerging regulation, and formal processes involving organizational ethics reviews that are meant to provide an assessment of adherence to the code of conduct, as well as an assessment of present and future risks involved with research proposals [101, 102, 103, 104].
items.zh: 核心伦理原则正被新兴法规和涉及组织伦理审查的正式流程所补充，这些审查旨在评估对行为准则的遵守情况，以及评估研究提案当前和未来的风险[101, 102, 103, 104]。

items.en: The acceleration of science through AI, especially with advanced agentic AI systems, requires advances in science and AI ethics policy and regulation [105, 106]. This adaptation is crucial to address the changing research landscape and the unique risks associated with AI agents of varying capabilities and autonomy.
items.zh: 通过 AI 加速科学，特别是使用先进的智能体式 AI 系统，需要科学和 AI 伦理政策与法规的进步[105, 106]。这种适应对于应对不断变化的研究格局以及与不同能力和自主性的 AI 智能体相关的独特风险至关重要。

items.en: Advancements in AI systems, like the co-scientist, require moving beyond the limited ethical considerations designed for earlier, specialized AI models with restricted application and action spaces [107]. Some preliminary frameworks have developed to understand the impact of LLM agents in science, specifically mapping risks across user intent, domain, and broader impact [108].
items.zh: 像共同科学家这样的 AI 系统的进步，需要超越为早期、应用和行动空间受限的专业 AI 模型设计的有限伦理考虑[107]。一些初步框架已经开发出来，用于理解 LLM 智能体在科学中的影响，特别是跨用户意图、领域和更广泛影响映射风险[108]。

items.en: Beyond the scientific domain, broad frameworks are being developed for evaluating the emergence of potentially dangerous capabilities in AI agents [109, 110, 111]. These frameworks assess capabilities related to persuasion, deception, cybersecurity, self-proliferation, and self-reasoning. As AI agents advance, safety evaluations in science must integrate these broader assessments. A long-term risk is that agentic systems could develop intrinsic goals influencing research directions. Human susceptibility to AI manipulation, already observed in other contexts [112], underscores the need for robust frameworks ensuring instruction-following and value alignment.
items.zh: 在科学领域之外，正在开发评估 AI 智能体潜在危险能力涌现的广泛框架[109, 110, 111]。这些框架评估与说服、欺骗、网络安全、自我扩散和自我推理相关的能力。随着 AI 智能体的进步，科学中的安全评估必须整合这些更广泛的评估。一个长期风险是智能体式系统可能发展出影响研究方向的内在目标。人类对 AI 操纵的易感性（已在其他背景下观察到[112]）强调了确保指令遵循和价值对齐的稳健框架的必要性。

items.en: More immediately on a shorter time-scale, technical safeguards are needed to address unethical research queries, malicious user intent, and the potential for extracting dangerous or dual-use knowledge from scientific AI systems. Because verification is computationally ‘easier’ than generation, significant research focuses on using advanced LLMs as ‘critics’ or ‘judges’ to evaluate both user queries and AI outputs acting as a scalable oversight mechanism. These critics operate based on predefined criteria, provided through direct instructions, examples (few-shot or many-shot prompting), or fine-tuning [113, 114, 115, 116, 117, 118]. They can also leverage external tools for grounding [119] and have shown promise in multimodal scenarios [120]. However, limitations remain; human expert involvement is crucial, as LLMs may not align with human judgment in specialized domains [121].
items.zh: 在更短的时间尺度上，需要技术保障措施来处理不道德的研究查询、恶意用户意图，以及从科学 AI 系统中提取危险或双重用途知识的可能性。由于验证在计算上比生成“更容易”，大量研究集中于使用高级 LLM 作为“批评者”或“评判者”来评估用户查询和 AI 输出，作为可扩展监督机制。这些批评者基于预定义标准运作，通过直接指令、示例（少样本或多样本提示）或微调提供[113, 114, 115, 116, 117, 118]。它们还可以利用外部工具进行接地[119]，并在多模态场景中显示出前景[120]。然而，局限性仍然存在；人类专家的参与至关重要，因为 LLM 在专业领域可能无法与人类判断对齐[121]。

items.en: Recognizing and mitigating adversarial attacks is a crucial, ongoing research area in the development of foundation models and advanced AI assistants [122, 85, 123, 124, 125, 126, 127, 128]. While manual red teaming has identified vulnerabilities, automated approaches now allow for optimizing prompt suffixes to bypass safety measures, using techniques like greedy, gradient-based, or evolutionary methods [129, 130]. Attacks can also exploit few-shot demonstrations, in-context learning [131, 132], and multimodal inputs [133]. Furthermore, LLMs can be used to generate and refine attacks against other LLMs [134], and attacks can be iterative, spanning multiple steps [135]. Defenses are being developed to counter both human and automated attacks, which is increasingly important in an agentic AI future [136].
items.zh: 识别和缓解对抗性攻击是基础模型和高级 AI 助手开发中一个关键且持续的研究领域[122, 85, 123, 124, 125, 126, 127, 128]。虽然手动红队测试已识别出漏洞，但自动化方法现在允许通过使用贪婪、基于梯度或进化方法等技术优化提示后缀以绕过安全措施[129, 130]。攻击还可以利用少样本演示、上下文学习[131, 132]和多模态输入[133]。此外，LLM 可用于生成和优化针对其他 LLM 的攻击[134]，并且攻击可以是迭代的，跨越多个步骤[135]。防御措施正在开发中，以应对人类和自动化攻击，这在智能体式 AI 的未来中日益重要[136]。

items.en: Advances in post-training of base models will likely improve overall adversarial robustness. However, domain-specific recognition of malicious use may still require dedicated development and integration into scientific AI assistants. In AI systems employing iterative reasoning (e.g., request interpretation, hypothesis generation, internal thoughts, evaluation, user queries), each component must be tested independently. This comprehensive testing should account for all potential failure modes, including the handling of unsafe queries, the safety of hypotheses (intermediate and final), and the accuracy of internal checks and filters.
items.zh: 基础模型后训练的进步可能会提高整体对抗鲁棒性。然而，对恶意使用的领域特定识别可能仍需要专门开发并集成到科学 AI 助手中。在使用迭代推理（例如，请求解释、假设生成、内部思考、评估、用户查询）的 AI 系统中，每个组件都必须独立测试。这种全面测试应考虑所有可能的故障模式，包括不安全查询的处理、假设（中间和最终）的安全性，以及内部检查和过滤器的准确性。

items.en: Scientific AI assistants, like the co-scientist, require integrated, configurable guidelines within their safeguards. Developers should anticipate the complexity of this challenge and prioritize flexible safeguarding to rapidly incorporate community feedback. These semantic safeguards may need to be augmented by traditional software safety measures, including trusted testers, gradual feature rollouts, access controls, request logging, and flagging uncertain outputs for manual review.
items.zh: 像共同科学家这样的科学 AI 助手需要在其保障措施中集成可配置的指南。开发者应预见这一挑战的复杂性，并优先考虑灵活的保障措施以快速纳入社区反馈。这些语义保障措施可能需要通过传统软件安全措施来增强，包括可信测试者、逐步功能推出、访问控制、请求日志记录，以及标记不确定输出以供人工审查。

items.en: Ensuring the safety of these systems, in line with existing AI safety guidelines [137, 138], necessitates a multi-pronged approach. This includes:
items.zh: 确保这些系统的安全性，符合现有的 AI 安全指南[137, 138]，需要多管齐下的方法。这包括：

items.en: Comprehensive threat modeling to identify potential vulnerabilities.
items.zh: 全面的威胁建模以识别潜在漏洞。

items.en: Defense mechanisms for each identified threat.
items.zh: 针对每个已识别威胁的防御机制。

items.en: Extensive red-teaming and security testing.
items.zh: 广泛的红队测试和安全测试。

items.en: Rapid response procedures for quick resolution of issues including vulnerability patches.
items.zh: 快速响应程序，用于快速解决问题，包括漏洞修补。

items.en: Continuous monitoring and performance tracking.
items.zh: 持续监控和性能跟踪。

items.en: These considerations highlight the need for responsible development, governance and careful deployment of technologies designed for advancing science, appropriate safeguards and ethical guidelines and close compliance with applicable regulations. They also further underscore the need for broad community engagement and an inclusive development of best practices and recommendations around safe and ethical use for AI in science.
items.zh: 这些考虑凸显了负责任开发、治理和谨慎部署旨在推进科学的技术、适当的保障措施和伦理指南以及严格遵守适用法规的必要性。它们还进一步强调了广泛社区参与和包容性开发关于人工智能在科学中安全与伦理使用的最佳实践和建议的必要性。

items.en: To mitigate these risks, the AI co-scientist currently employs the following safety mechanisms:
items.zh: 为减轻这些风险，AI 联合科学家目前采用以下安全机制：

items.en: Reliance on public frontier LLMs. The system utilizes established public Gemini 2.0 models, which already incorporate extensive safety evaluation and safeguards.
items.zh: 依赖公共前沿大语言模型。该系统利用已建立的公共 Gemini 2.0 模型，这些模型已包含广泛的安全评估和保障措施。

items.en: Initial research goal safety review. Upon input, each research goal undergoes automated safety evaluation. Goals deemed potentially unsafe are rejected.
items.zh: 初始研究目标安全审查。输入时，每个研究目标都经过自动安全评估。被认为可能不安全的目标被拒绝。

items.en: Research hypothesis safety review. Generated hypotheses are reviewed for safety, even when the overarching research goal is deemed safe. Potentially unsafe hypotheses are excluded from the tournament, not developed any further, and are not presented to the user.
items.zh: 研究假设安全审查。生成的假设会进行安全审查，即使总体研究目标被认为是安全的。可能不安全的假设被排除在锦标赛之外，不再进一步开发，也不会呈现给用户。

items.en: Continuous monitoring of research directions. A meta-review agent provides an overview of research directions, enabling the AI co-scientist to continuously monitor for potential safety concerns and alert users if a research direction is detected as being potentially unsafe.
items.zh: 持续监控研究方向。一个元审查代理提供研究方向的概览，使 AI 联合科学家能够持续监控潜在安全问题，并在检测到研究方向可能不安全时提醒用户。

items.en: Explainability and transparency. All system components, including the safety review, provide not only the final recommendation but also a detailed reasoning trace that can be used to justify and audit system decisions.
items.zh: 可解释性和透明度。所有系统组件，包括安全审查，不仅提供最终建议，还提供详细的推理轨迹，可用于证明和审计系统决策。

items.en: Comprehensive logging. All system activities are logged and stored for future analysis and auditing.
items.zh: 全面日志记录。所有系统活动都被记录并存储，用于未来分析和审计。

items.en: Safety evaluations and red teaming. A preliminary red teaming effort has been undertaken to ensure that the current implementation of unsafe research goal detection is robust and accurate. This evaluation includes an assessment of the system behavior when presented with 1,200 adversarial research goals across 40 distinct topic areas as discussed in Section˜4.4.
items.zh: 安全评估和红队测试。已进行初步红队测试，以确保当前不安全研究目标检测的实现是稳健和准确的。该评估包括对系统在 40 个不同主题领域（如第 4.4 节所述）中呈现 1,200 个对抗性研究目标时的行为评估。

items.en: Trusted tester program. We are enthused by the early promise of the AI co-scientist system and believe it is important to more rigorously understand its strengths and limitations in many more areas of science and biomedicine; alongside making the system available to many more researchers who it is intended to support and assist. To facilitate this responsibly and with rigour, we will be enabling access to the system for scientists through a Trusted Tester Program to gather real-world feedback on the utility and robustness of the system.
items.zh: 可信测试者计划。我们对 AI 联合科学家系统的早期前景感到振奋，并认为在更多科学和生物医学领域更严格地理解其优势和局限性非常重要；同时使该系统可供更多它旨在支持和协助的研究人员使用。为负责任且严谨地促进这一点，我们将通过可信测试者计划让科学家访问该系统，以收集关于系统实用性和稳健性的真实世界反馈。

items.en: Crucially, the AI co-scientist is designed to operate with continuous human expert oversight, ensuring that final decisions are always made by scientists exercising their expert judgment.
items.zh: 关键的是，AI 联合科学家被设计为在持续的人类专家监督下运行，确保最终决策始终由行使专家判断的科学家做出。

full.secZh: 7 未来工作

items.en: The AI co-scientist is in its early development, with many opportunities for improvement. Immediate improvement opportunities include enhanced literature reviews, cross-checks with external tools, improved factuality checking, and increased citation recall to minimize missed relevant research. Coherence checks would also improve the system by reducing the burden of reviewing flawed hypotheses.
items.zh: AI 联合科学家处于早期开发阶段，有许多改进机会。直接的改进机会包括增强文献综述、与外部工具的交叉检查、改进事实核查以及增加引用召回率以尽量减少遗漏相关研究。一致性检查也将通过减少审查有缺陷假设的负担来改进系统。

items.en: Developing more objective evaluation metrics, potentially incorporating automated literature-based validation and simulated experiments, is a key area. Methods to mitigate biases or error patterns inherited from the base LLMs are also important, alongside analysis of the complementarity and optimal combination of different agent components.
items.zh: 开发更客观的评估指标，可能结合自动化的基于文献的验证和模拟实验，是一个关键领域。减轻从基础大语言模型继承的偏见或错误模式的方法也很重要，同时分析不同智能体组件的互补性和最优组合。

items.en: A critical need is a larger-scale evaluation involving more subject matter experts with diverse, high-resolution research goals. Stress-testing the system at every level of resolution (from disease mechanisms to protein design, and expanding to other scientific disciplines) will reveal further areas for improvement. Finally, since laboratory resources are limited, improved evaluation frameworks could assist with hypothesis selection.
items.zh: 一个关键需求是进行更大规模的评估，涉及更多具有多样化、高分辨率研究目标的主题专家。在每一级分辨率（从疾病机制到蛋白质设计，并扩展到其他科学学科）对系统进行压力测试将揭示更多改进领域。最后，由于实验室资源有限，改进的评估框架可以帮助假设选择。

items.en: Several opportunities remain to expand co-scientist’s capabilities. Reinforcement learning could enhance hypothesis ranking, proposal generation, and evolutionary refinement.
items.zh: 仍有几个机会扩展联合科学家的能力。强化学习可以增强假设排序、提案生成和进化优化。

items.en: Currently, the system assesses text from open-access publications but not images, data sets, or major public databases. Integrating these publicly available resources would significantly enhance the co-scientist’s ability to generate and justify proposed hypotheses.
items.zh: 目前，系统评估来自开放获取出版物的文本，但不评估图像、数据集或主要公共数据库。整合这些公共可用资源将显著增强联合科学家生成和论证所提假设的能力。

items.en: Future work will focus on handling more complex experimental designs, such as multi-step experiments and those involving conditional logic. Integrating co-scientist with laboratory automation systems could potentially create a closed-loop for validation and a grounded basis for iterative improvement. Exploring more structured user interfaces for providing feedback and insights from targeted user research studies, beyond free text, could improve the efficiency of human-AI collaboration in this paradigm.
items.zh: 未来工作将专注于处理更复杂的实验设计，例如多步骤实验和涉及条件逻辑的实验。将联合科学家与实验室自动化系统集成可能创建一个用于验证的闭环和迭代改进的坚实基础。探索更结构化的用户界面，用于从目标用户研究研究中提供反馈和见解，超越自由文本，可以提高这种范式中人机协作的效率。

full.secZh: 8 讨论

items.en: Our study represents an initial foray into accelerating novel scientific discovery with agentic AI systems and here, we discuss some of the broader implications. The co-scientist iteratively refines its generated hypotheses through a generate, debate, evolve” approach with specialized agents under the hood. This design creates a self-improving cycle for research hypothesis generation, as measured by automated evaluation metrics, and showcases the potential benefits of test-time compute scaling for scientific reasoning.
items.zh: 我们的研究是使用智能体式AI系统加速新颖科学发现的初步尝试，在此我们讨论一些更广泛的影响。该联合科学家通过“生成、辩论、演化”的方法，在底层使用专门智能体，迭代优化其生成的假设。这种设计为研究假设生成创建了一个自我改进的循环（通过自动评估指标衡量），并展示了测试时算力扩展对科学推理的潜在益处。

items.en: Instead of brute-force generation of a vast number of hypotheses and relying on volume to chance into a few potentially useful ones, the system is designed to mimic key aspects of the scientific reasoning method in an intelligent manner. As detailed in Section˜3, the co-scientist employs principled internal mechanisms, including scientific debates, tournaments, iterative refinement, and human feedback loops to progressively improve the quality of its proposals, and converge on high quality and well-reasoned hypotheses.
items.zh: 该系统并非暴力生成大量假设并依赖数量偶然获得少数有用假设，而是设计为以智能方式模仿科学推理方法的关键方面。如第3节所述，联合科学家采用原则性内部机制，包括科学辩论、锦标赛、迭代优化和人类反馈循环，逐步提高其提案的质量，并收敛到高质量且推理充分的假设。

items.en: The AI co-scientist facilitates the generation of novel scientific hypotheses and uncovering new insights by synthesizing extensive literature and identifying latent relationships. While its primary utility in its current form may lie in enabling more incremental advancements — such as the computational repurposing of existing therapeutics — it may also be able to support exploratory, breakthrough research. When researchers define such open-ended research goals requiring complex and out-of-the box thinking, the system may produce outputs of varying confidence. Therefore, rigorous validation and critical appraisal by domain experts remain paramount. This system is intended to augment, not supplant, human scientific reasoning, empowering researchers to accelerate discovery while maintaining intellectual control over the generated insights. We further expand on the novelty aspects in the specific context of the applications considered in this work in LABEL:sec:glossary.
items.zh: AI联合科学家通过综合大量文献和识别潜在关系，促进新颖科学假设的生成和新见解的发现。虽然其当前形式的主要效用可能在于实现更渐进的进步——例如现有治疗方法的计算性重新利用——但它也可能支持探索性、突破性的研究。当研究人员定义需要复杂和非常规思维的开放式研究目标时，系统可能产生置信度各异的输出。因此，领域专家的严格验证和批判性评估仍然至关重要。该系统旨在增强而非取代人类的科学推理，使研究人员能够加速发现，同时保持对生成见解的智力控制。我们在LABEL:sec:glossary中进一步扩展了本工作所考虑应用的具体背景下的新颖性方面。

items.en: Notably, this work demonstrates the validation of co-scientist hypotheses via experimental findings in multiple laboratories. In drug repurposing, co-scientist identifies novel candidates for AML that demonstrated in vitro efficacy at clinically relevant concentrations, including the identification of new repurposing opportunities beyond current preclinical knowledge. For liver fibrosis, the co-scientist proposes new epigenetic treatment targets, with subsequent in vitro experiments validating the anti-fibrotic activity of several suggested compounds, including an FDA-approved drug. In the realm of antimicrobial resistance, the co-scientist independently recapitulates a novel, unpublished finding regarding the mechanism of cf-PICI transfer between bacterial species. Early results over several queries of varying scientific complexity suggests the co-scientist has a potential to contribute to discovery within various biomedical domains.
items.zh: 值得注意的是，本工作通过多个实验室的实验发现验证了联合科学家的假设。在药物重新利用中，联合科学家识别出急性髓系白血病（AML）的新候选药物，这些药物在临床相关浓度下显示出体外疗效，包括识别出超出当前临床前知识的新重新利用机会。对于肝纤维化，联合科学家提出了新的表观遗传治疗靶点，随后的体外实验验证了几种建议化合物的抗纤维化活性，包括一种FDA批准的药物。在抗菌素耐药性领域，联合科学家独立重现了一个关于cf-PICI在细菌物种间转移机制的新颖未发表发现。对几个不同科学复杂性的查询的早期结果表明，联合科学家有潜力为各种生物医学领域的发现做出贡献。

items.en: In the experiments reported here, the co-scientist did not require specialized pre-training, post-training, or a reinforcement learning framework. It leverages the capabilities of existing base LLMs, potentially benefiting from updates to those models without requiring retraining of the co-scientist system itself, which presents advantages of compute efficiency and generalizability. The system’s architecture incorporates self-play, internal consistency checks, and tournament-based ranking, which support iterative hypothesis generation, evaluation, and refinement. This is reflected in the observed improvement in hypothesis quality over time. This self-evolution can be improved further by expanded tool use integration, including database queries, allowing the co-scientist to ground its proposals in existing knowledge and identify novel connections. In the future, we may leverage data and tournament ranking generated by the co-scientist itself as feedback to improve the whole system using reinforcement learning.
